title: Crusea megalocarpa
taxonomy: Crusea megalocarpa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the madder or coffee family due to its various species that produce useful compounds. Crusea megalocarpa is native to Central America and has been found to grow in tropical and subtropical regions. It belongs to the Rubieae tribe, which comprises many species of flowering plants. Further classification of Crusea megalocarpa includes its genus, Crusea, and its order, Gentianales.
